fields.title: The Corner
fields.body: You're on your corner. A payphone that never rings. A bodega with bars on the windows. The city moves around you like you're not there. For now, that suits you fine.

fields.title: The Board
fields.body: A corkboard inside Sal's back room. Folded notes, burner numbers, job descriptions written in the kind of language that doesn't hold up in court. Someone's moving product. Someone needs muscle. Someone wants a problem to disappear.

fields.title: Sal's Bar
fields.body: The kind of bar that doesn't have a sign. Sal keeps the lights low, the bottles cheap, and his mouth shut. Half the city's criminal ecosystem runs through this place on any given night.

fields.title: Badge Problem
fields.body: He followed you from the corner. Off-duty, no radio. Just him and his reputation. He wants a piece of whatever you just collected.

fields.title: No Badge Saves You Here
fields.body: He's face-down on asphalt. You strip his wallet of the bribe money and disappear before anyone notices. Word will get around.

fields.title: Ray's Dry Cleaners
fields.body:
Ray Kowalski in the back of a dry cleaners he definitely doesn't own.
He slides a napkin across the table. The name is Marty. Get $800, keep 20%.
He adds "don't make a mess" in a way that suggests it hasn't worked before.
A second napkin sits on the table as you leave. Ray doesn't mention it.
Same street as Marty, different unit.

fields.title: The Walkup
fields.body: The building super is mopping the lobby. He's seen people like you before.

fields.title: Marty
fields.body:
Third floor. Elevator broken. Marty answers in a bathrobe at 2pm. Kind face.
Kid's drawings on the fridge. He had the money three weeks ago but his
daughter's car broke down. He has $200 now. He'll have the rest in two weeks.
His phone rings. He silences it. "That's my daughter. I'll call her back."

fields.title: The Muscle
fields.body: Marty goes to the bedroom. Player waits with the kid drawings.

fields.title: Loading Dock
fields.body: The warehouse sits dark at the end of Pier 9. Two of Deluca's guys lean against the loading dock, smoking. They're not expecting trouble. They're also not moving.

fields.title: Dennis
fields.body:
Dennis is outside a laundromat he definitely doesn't own. He's wearing a suit
that's one size too small and sweating despite it being November. He explains
the job in the vaguest possible terms. The package sits between you on a
plastic chair. It makes no sound. It does nothing. It is simply there.

fields.title: Dock Fight
fields.body: The first guy reaches for something in his jacket. You don't give him the chance.

fields.title: The Package
fields.body:
You are alone with it on the bus. It's heavier than it looks. Taped perfectly.
No label. Slightly warm. The city moves past the windows. You have a moment.

fields.title: Side Entrance
fields.body: The guards don't even count the money before stepping aside. One of them points you toward a service entrance on the far side of the dock. 'Stay out of the back office,' he says. You note that.

fields.title: The Drop
fields.body:
An apartment building. Third floor. Unit 307. Knock twice.
Gloria answers immediately, like she was waiting behind the door. She takes
the package without looking at it. She looks at you instead, for slightly too
long. She hands you an envelope and closes the door.

fields.title: Inside the Warehouse
fields.body: Floor-to-ceiling shelving. The place is a maze. At the far end, two paths: one leads toward voices and the glow of a work lamp — someone's still here. The other leads toward rows of padlocked crates.

fields.title: Back to Normal
fields.body:
You are back where you started. Dennis is not there. The laundromat is empty.
His coffee cup is still on the windowsill. Still warm. No further explanation.

fields.title: Taken
fields.body: They're better than they looked. You wake up zip-tied to a chair in the back office. By the time someone cuts you loose, the warehouse is empty.

fields.title: Clean Exit
fields.body: Nothing incriminating. Nothing worth the risk. You document what you see and walk out the way you came. Sometimes the job is just reconnaissance.

fields.title: The Accountant
fields.body: The man with the ledgers turns out to be Deluca's bookkeeper. He's skimming. You now know things about Deluca's operation that Deluca doesn't know you know. That's worth more than anything in those crates.

fields.title: The Score
fields.body: Pharmacy-grade opioids. Twelve crates, all tagged for a hospital system that'll never see them. You make two calls. By morning, the crates are gone and you're owed a favor by the people who moved them.

fields.title: The Corner
fields.body: You find him on his usual corner, back to the wall, watching the street. He clocks you and his jaw tightens. You can step to him or let it go.

fields.title: Squaring Up
fields.body: He comes off the wall fast. Hands up, eyes wild. This is happening.

fields.title: The Real Problem
fields.body: The corner boy's down, but a door opens across the street. Big guy, slow walk. He's been watching. Now it's his turn.

fields.title: Paid in Full
fields.body: The envelope is in your pocket. They'll think twice before shorting you again. You walk back to the square.

fields.title: Left on the Pavement
fields.body: You come to against a dumpster. Wallet's gone. Ribs ache. You limp back to the square with nothing.

fields.title: Not Today
fields.body: You hold his gaze for a moment, then turn and walk. Some debts aren't worth the trouble. Today.

fields.title: The Case
fields.body: Russ finds you outside Sal's. He's holding a slim aluminum case and an unlit cigarette. Ten percent to walk something to a laundromat on Fifth. Hand it to a man in a gray jacket. Don't look inside.

fields.title: Name Your Price
fields.body: Russ doesn't like it. He chews the unlit cigarette and does the math behind his eyes. You wait. He either agrees or you walk, and you both know it.

fields.title: Third Street Bridge
fields.body: Halfway across the bridge, you pick up a tail. Dark coat, measured pace. Could be nothing. The laundromat is two blocks further.

fields.title: Clean Exchange
fields.body: Eleven seconds. Gray jacket takes the case, hands you an envelope, walks. You don't watch him go. You count the bills two blocks later. Everything's there. Russ's cut is light. Yours isn't.

fields.title: Made
fields.body: The tail is a vice detective with a long memory. You ditch the case in a trash can and spend an hour proving you were just walking. By the time you're clear, the buyer is gone. So is your cut.

fields.title: Eighteen Percent
fields.body: Russ paid eighteen percent without much argument. The walk was clean, the handoff was fast, and you're back at the corner in under two hours. Some jobs go the way they're supposed to.

fields.title: Crescent Street
fields.body: You find the captain finishing his rounds at the bodega on Crescent. Two runners flank him, but he sends them off when he clocks you coming. He's not worried. He should think harder about that.

fields.title: Squaring Off
fields.body: He moves first — a short jab that says he does this for a living. You settle in.

fields.title: Reminder
fields.body: He's better than you today. You end up against the bodega's shuttered front while he steps over you and tells his runners to move it. He doesn't even take your wallet. Worse — he doesn't need to.

fields.title: The Talk
fields.body: You lay it out. His crew is running tax on the wrong side of Crescent, and that ends tonight. He listens. Whether he thinks you're the kind of person who can back that up is another question.

fields.title: No Blood
fields.body: He measures you for a long moment, then tells his runners to move it. They go. No blood, no debt — just a line redrawn without anyone having to fall. The bodega owner nods at you through the window.
